title: U.S. Fast-Tracks Arms Deals Valued at $8.6 Billion to Mideast Partners
summary: The Persian Gulf countries and Israel have faced repeated Iranian attacks during the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran. The State Department move bypassed congressional review.

title: U.S. to Withdraw 5,000 Troops From Germany. But Thousands Will Remain.
summary: The U.S. military has tens of thousands of personnel in Germany, more than in any other foreign country but Japan. Their significance extends well beyond Europe.

title: How Germany May Have Misjudged Trump’s Anger on Iran
summary: Germany had appeared not to believe President Trump’s threats to pull troops from the country. Once it was announced, Berlin offered a measured response.

title: Hegseth’s and Moulton’s Parallel Lives Collide Over Iran
summary: The defense secretary and Representative Seth Moulton, a Democrat, debated the Iran war through the prism of their shared experiences fighting Iraq.

title: Trump’s Push for Electoral Retribution Heads to the Ballot Box
summary: The president’s push to punish political enemies in his own party will play a starring role in a series of Republican primaries this month in which he has backed challengers.

title: Trump’s Vision for D.C. ‘Garden of Heroes’ Statues Grows in Size and Cost
summary: Plans for the project include formal gardens, reflecting pools and plazas alongside the statues of 250 notable Americans, according to documents obtained by The New York Times.

title: Everyone Who Could Be in Trump’s ‘Garden of Heroes’
summary: President Trump plans to build a park along the Potomac River featuring life-size statues of 250 Americans.

title: Mace, Boebert and Luna Want Congress Members to Pay a Price for Sexual Abuse. Will It Work?
summary: Three Republican women who had a hand in forcing the resignations of lawmakers accused of sexual misconduct said they are out to name and shame more offenders. It is not clear how far they will go.

title: Spirit Airlines Shuts Down After Years of Struggle
summary: Spirit once upended the industry by offering very low fares but was in its second bankruptcy in two years after years of struggle.

title: Spirit Airlines Is Shutting Down. Here’s What to Do if You’re Scheduled to Fly.
summary: After two stints in bankruptcy, the low-cost airline shut down early Saturday. Here’s what travelers should know about rebooking and refunds.

title: Supreme Court Asked to Restore Access to Abortion Pill by Mail
summary: A federal appeals court temporarily halted a Food and Drug Administration regulation that has greatly expanded access to the abortion pill mifepristone.

title: Abortion Providers Forced to Adapt After Court Ruling Blocks Pill Access by Mail
summary: The Fifth Circuit Court’s ruling, which is being appealed, reinstates a requirement that patients visit a health care provider in person to obtain mifepristone, upending abortion access in the United States.

title: Tucker Carlson Discusses Breaking With Trump, the Iran War and Antisemitism
summary: The conservative media commentator split with the administration over the war in Iran. Will the breakup last?

summary: “The Interview” host Lulu Garcia-Navarro talks about her preparation to interview Mr. Carlson and some judgment calls she had to make in real time.

title: Ashley McBryde Saved Her Own Life. Now She’s Rocking Out Her Way.
summary: The prolific country singer and songwriter has never quite fit the country music mold. Her upcoming album, “Wild,” revels in her individuality, and her sobriety.

summary: Ahead of the 152nd annual horse race at Churchill Downs, attendees trotted out some eye-catching ensembles.

summary: How often do you talk to strangers? What’s stopping you?

title: Can a Second-Home Tax Work in New York? The Numbers Don’t Add Up Yet.
summary: The tax proposal is being held up as a generational attempt to make the ultrawealthy pay more to society and potentially raise $500 million a year for New York City.

title: Shipwreck Reveals Fate of Vanished World War I Coast Guard Cutter
summary: The Tampa disappeared in 1918 with 131 British and American personnel and civilians aboard. It was the largest single American naval combat loss of life in World War I

title: After Trump’s Ousting of Maduro, Little Has Changed for Most Venezuelans
summary: U.S. officials say they will “unleash prosperity” by commandeering the oil industry. Many people in Caracas say it will take far more than that.

title: Steve Clark, Olympic Swimmer Shadowed by Depression, Dies at 82
summary: A three-time gold medalist in the 1960s, he was a champion when athletes were expected to be stoic and vulnerability was viewed as a sign of mental weakness.
